div1.entryFree:
Barely, 1) in a state of nakedness: when you have our roses, you b. leave our thorns to prick ourselves, and mock us with our bareness, All's IV, 2, 19 (cf. coldly, Hml. I, 2, 181; grossly, III, 3, 80).
2) merely, only: shall I not have b. my principal? Merch. IV, 1, 342. R2 II, 1, 226. Cymb. II, 4, 7.
